context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=7; meal=bite-sized sandwiches; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., watching TV on the left or working on a laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE observed left-attention meals with these foods showed the user explicitly preferred hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-TV noon meals with non-dip lunch foods showed a preference for substantially hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN 1 min BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals also supported the quieter default in some non-dip cases.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or visual focus in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals show the user prefers preserving the forward view. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other visual focus on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side view.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN slower than default BECAUSE the user selected medium in one left-attention meal and slow in another, indicating a consistent preference to reduce motion intensity when attention is shared with a left-side activity and the food may be messier. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce or dip THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly chose the slowest speed in that observed context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN slow or medium, with evidence now favoring slower motion for more involved lunch foods BECAUSE front-TV meals with chicken-and-broccoli and bite-sized sandwiches used medium and slow rather than fast. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN fast BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals supported efficient motion in simpler forward-attention contexts.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food is a general meal item without evidence of mouth-opening or bite-shape concerns THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this was selected across most observed meals, indicating a general preference for reliable bite acquisition. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the new observed sandwich meal shows the user accepted a narrower-bite strategy for that food type.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE observed left-attention meals with messier food conditions show the user wanted extra verification. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals show the user also wants extra confirmation for some non-dip entrées. IF dining context is quiet, social/front-attention, or front-TV AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special messiness or lunch-style complexity signal THEN no BECAUSE several earlier non-messy meals supported a lower-interaction workflow when extra verification was not needed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's lack of trunk control prevents leaning forward comfortably, and all observed selections are consistent with this physical constraint.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer in any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer, which is incompatible with the user's physical limitations and unsupported by observed selections.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user preferred a more salient multimodal cue while attention was divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals indicate the user wants stronger cues when engaged with forward media during more involved foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the simpler visual cue.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across social, front-attention, and left-attention meals. This indicates a stable preference for minimizing effort over more explicit initiation methods.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user preferred a more noticeable multimodal completion cue while attention was divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals indicate the user wants stronger completion cues in that context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the simpler visual completion cue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, and button use is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND drinking transfer occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE front-TV meals show a stable low-effort preference with context-specific flexibility between passive sensing and timeout-based progression. IF dining context is quiet or left-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain split between perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND mouth wiping occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE front-TV meals show a stable low-effort preference with flexibility in how completion is recognized. IF dining context is quiet or left-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain split between perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to external media or work (e.g., TV on the left, laptop on the left, or TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN yes BECAUSE across observed media-attention meals the user chose retraction, consistent with prioritizing reduced visual obstruction when attending to a screen. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted less interruption and shorter meal time in that meal.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauce or dip available AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping in that meal, consistent with minimizing mess in social settings. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user preferred some dipping but not heavy dipping.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show a strong preference for ample time to intervene when attention is divided and the food may be messier. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory mixed entrée without dipping THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE one front-TV entrée meal showed the user wanted a very long intervention window for that more involved lunch food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches without dipping THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the new front-TV sandwich meal shows the user preferred quick progression for that easier handoff pattern. IF dining context is social with a person in front or includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the shortest wait, indicating preference for quick progression in simpler contexts.
